mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal's Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting equity valuations and generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL growth stays below 0.3% and INDPRO rises, or the regime filter fails to improve out-of-sample hit rate by at least 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a continuous floor for consumer spending expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following week.
failure_condition: The monthly payroll series shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested rolling windows, or positive payroll months yield negative median returns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and falling unemployment signals a tightening cycle that eventually compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction model fails to predict negative 60-day returns when FEDFUNDS rises and UNRATE falls, or the combined signal's out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.02 over a 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction below zero percent confirms economic recession, triggering immediate portfolio deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of quarters where GDPC1 growth turns negative, or the signal's predictive accuracy does not exceed random chance (50%) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during high-inflation environments indicate stagflationary supply shocks, forcing rapid equity sell-offs and generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.4% monthly growth and INDPRO falls, or the regime-specific hit rate remains below 45% over a 5-year evaluation period.
